options.B: Reviewing and validating the generated output for accuracy and completeness.
options.C: GitHub Copilot Chat uses a static database for generating explanations.
options.D: The explanations are primarily derived from user-provided documentation.

question: How can you use GitHub Copilot to get inline suggestions for refactoring your code? (Choose two.)

options.A: By adding comments to your code and triggering a suggestion.
options.B: By highlighting the code you want to fix, right-clicking, and selecting "Fix using GitHub Copilot."
options.C: By running the gh copilot fix command.
options.D: By using the "/fix" command in GitHub Copilot in-line chat.
options.E: By highlighting the code, you want to fix, right-clicking, and selecting "Refactor using GitHub Copilot."

question: How can GitHub Copilot be limited when it comes to suggesting unit tests?

options.A: GitHub copilot can generate all types of unit tests, including those for edge cases and complex integration scenarios
options.B: GitHub Copilot primarily suggests basic unit tests that focus on core functionalities, often requiring additional input from developers for comprehensive coverage.
options.C: GitHub Copilot can handle any complexity in code and automatically generate appropriate unit tests.
options.D: GitHub Copilot's limitations in generating unit tests can vary based on the IDE version you are using.

question: How can the concept of fairness be integrated into the process of operating an AI tool?

options.A: Focusing on accessibility will ensure fairness.
options.B: Focusing on collecting large datasets for training will ensure fairness.

question: What is a limitation of content exclusions?

options.A: Content exclusions can be worked around as it is only available for Git repositories.
options.B: Content exclusions can only be configured by an enterprise administrator.
options.C: Content exclusions are only available in the GitHub Copilot individual plan.
options.D: Repository administrators and organization owners cannot manage content exclusion settings.

question: How does GitHub Copilot suggest code optimizations for improved performance?

options.A: By analyzing the codebase and suggesting more efficient algorithms or data structures.
options.B: By automatically rewriting the codebase to use more efficient code.
options.C: By enforcing strict coding standards that ensure optimal performance.

options.A: "Show me how to improve the readability of this function."
options.B: "Suggest ways to enhance the maintainability of this code segment."
options.C: "Refactor my application meet the latest coding standards."
options.D: "Predict future coding trends and update my codebase accordingly."

question: What is zero-shot prompting?

options.A: Giving as little context to GitHub Copilot as possible
options.B: Telling GitHub Copilot it needs to show only the correct answer
options.C: Only giving GitHub Copilot a question as a prompt and no examples
options.D: Giving GitHub Copilot examples of the problem you want to solve
options.E: Giving GitHub Copilot examples of the algorithm and outcome you want to use

question: What do you check when GitHub Copilot content exclusions are not working? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. (Choose two.)

options.A: If the user is in an organization that has content exclusions configured.
options.B: If the content exclusion settings changed in the last 30 minutes or before that.
options.C: If GitHub Copilot can connect to the server selected in your user settings.
options.D: If the user is part of the content exclusion team that limits the use of content exclusions.

question: What is a likely effect of GitHub Copilot being trained on commonly used code patterns?

options.A: Suggest homogeneous solutions if provided a diverse data set.
options.B: Suggest innovative coding solutions that are not yet popular.
options.C: Suggest code snippets that reflect the most common practices in the training data.

question: Which of the following describes role prompting?
